Class PIIMatch

java.lang.Object
com.semanticprivacyguard.model.PIIMatch
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<PIIMatch>
public final class PIIMatch extends Object implements Comparable<PIIMatch>
Represents a single detected occurrence of PII within the original text.

Instances are immutable and are created by detector implementations. A match records the exact character span [startIndex, endIndex), the raw matched value, the classified type, the detection source, and a confidence score in the range [0.0, 1.0].

PIIMatch m = new PIIMatch(PIIType.EMAIL, "alice@example.com",
                          10, 27, DetectionSource.HEURISTIC, 1.0);
System.out.println(m.getType());       // EMAIL
System.out.println(m.getStartIndex()); // 10
Since:
1.0.0
Author:
Hemant Naik

    compareTo(PIIMatch other)
    Orders matches by their start position, then by end position descending (longest match first for the same start).

  • Constructor Details

    • PIIMatch

      public PIIMatch(PIIType type, String value, int startIndex, int endIndex, PIIMatch.DetectionSource source, double confidence)
      Constructs a new PIIMatch.
      Parameters:
      type - the PII category (never null)
      value - the raw matched string (never null)
      startIndex - inclusive start offset in the original text
      endIndex - exclusive end offset in the original text
      source - how the match was found (never null)
      confidence - detection confidence in [0.0, 1.0]

    • length

      public int length()
      Returns the length of the matched span.

    • overlaps

      public boolean overlaps(PIIMatch other)
      Returns true if this match overlaps with other. Overlapping matches are de-duplicated by the composite detector.

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(PIIMatch other)
      Orders matches by their start position, then by end position descending (longest match first for the same start).
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<PIIMatch>
